TextENGNew: The die of fate... had been cast.
Had I let my emotions get the better of me... and ruined everything?
...Should I have kept my mouth shut... and left it all to Mion, who had been relatively composed?
...I remembered the curry insult punishment game, and how Satoko had told me to leave everything to her since me opening my mouth would only make things worse.
...Maybe Satoko had been right all along.
...Impulsive as I was, maybe I should have just stared at the floor in silence.
...But... it was too late for regrets.
...A die, once thrown, cannot be taken back.
All I could do was pray for a favorable outcome.
"...You did the best you could, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS03/02/140200104. So... leave the rest to the teachers now.@k@vS03/02/140200105. ...That's what I think, I think."

"The best I could...?@k@vS03/01/140100386. ...If I'd truly given it everything, desperately, with my life on the line... I would have spirited Satoko away last night and hidden her in some abandoned house.@k@vS03/01/140100387. ...The fact that I didn't means I wasn't really giving it my all."
changeReason: Stylistic rephrasing throughout; both old and new contain the full dialogue about giving it everything and the self-reproach about not having done so.

TextENGNew: "...............Stop overthinking it, okay?@k@vS03/02/140200107. ...You're pushing yourself too hard, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS03/02/140200108. ...And it's not just you—it's hard on all of us, you know?"
Just leave me alone.@k...With those words, I leaned back and stared blankly at the ceiling.
...Had I really gotten the situation across to Chie-sensei?
...Would the consultation center in Okinomiya actually intervene... and resolve everything today?
...Would they officially recognize the problem with the uncle... and take proper measures...?
...After lunch break, the afternoon classes were mostly self-study.
Sensei came back to the classroom now and then, but the phone in the teachers' lounge kept ringing, and she'd be on it for long stretches.
The rest of the class... seemed to have a vague sense of what those phone calls were about.
Sensei came trotting back to the classroom.

"...I'm truly sorry for all the interruptions with the phone calls today.@k@vS03/22/141400067. Let's wrap up for the day.@k@vS03/22/141400068. As usual, go straight home and don't wander around, all right?"
"All riiiiiiiiiiise!"
...From what I could see of Sensei's expression, the dark cloud that had settled over her face after lunch had never lifted.
...That was not the face of someone who could breathe a sigh of relief.
Of course it wasn't.
...Even if Sensei understood the situation perfectly and conveyed it as passionately as she could...@kthe final decision rested with the Okinomiya child consultation center.
...Sensei understood that too... which was why her expression looked so uncertain.
"Kei-chan, let's go home.@k@vS03/03/140300159. ...Nobody's in the mood for club activities, right?"
"Ahahaha.@k@vS03/02/140200110. Mii-chan, club isn't any fun when someone's missing."
...Rena was right.
...Without all of us together... nothing was any fun.
If only Sensei had worn a more reassuring expression... I could have headed home with a lighter heart.
...But I............couldn't let myself feel at ease yet.
...I couldn't afford to be naive and say 'the die is cast, so all I can do is pray.'
In case... they decided to 'wait and see' again...@k...I needed to take further action.
...And there was... one more place... worth appealing to.
.....................She might laugh me off...@k...or I might be right on the mark... and end up putting my own life in danger.
But... I would try.
"Oh... Mion.@k@vS03/01/140100389. Would it be okay if I stopped by your place today?@k@vS03/01/140100390. ...There's something I want to talk about."
Something in the way I'd said it must have tipped her off, because Mion seemed to grasp my real intent immediately.
...I wanted to talk to her alone.@k...That was the gist of it.
"Mii-chan, Keiichi-kun!@k@vS03/02/140200112. Let's hurry home~!"
Rena already had her shoes on and was waving from the schoolyard.
...Mion's house was... sprawling in every conceivable way.
The house itself had an old-fashioned, traditional gassho-style look, but the grounds—the sheer scale of the property—were enormous.
...I could easily see why people said they were the great landowners who held all of Hinamizawa in their grip.
Mion showed me to the guest room and had a servant bring tea.
"Wow.@k@vS03/01/140100393. You really are rich.@k@vS03/01/140100394. You even have housekeepers!"
"It's just me and Grandma living here.@k@vS03/03/140300163. I couldn't manage it alone."
The housekeeper announced that it was five o'clock and she'd be taking her leave, then quietly withdrew.
"They come two or three times a week to help look after Grandma and clean the house.@k@vS03/03/140300165. Three or four people rotate through the shifts.@k@vS03/03/140300166. They really help."
"Only until evening, though.@k@vS03/03/140300167. Oh, and when we have a banquet or need extra hands, we call them in separately."
...If you had the money to hire all these people, then why not just take Satoko in?
...I glared at her with that thought, and it seemed to reach Mion loud and clear.
"It's fine.@k@vS03/01/140100396. ...I know this isn't about money."
"...Let me say this upfront... my family might be wealthy, but...@k@vS03/03/140300170. ...I don't have the kind of money I can use freely."
...It was starting to look like I was bullying her.
...Mion had been apologizing to me constantly lately.
...Was she always the type to apologize this much...?
Or had my expression just been that severe recently...?
"You had something you wanted to talk about, didn't you, Kei-chan?"
"Mion, I have... something very serious to discuss.@k@vS03/01/140100398. ...It's going to be sudden, and you might be blindsided, but hear me out."
"Huh... W-What?"
"Oh... let me give you an escape route first.@k@vS03/01/140100400. ...Even if what I'm about to say turns out to be true, you don't have to admit it. I won't press you. That's fine."

"...But... I want you to listen to the end.@k@vS03/01/140100403. ...........As the heir to the Sonozaki family, the current foremost house among the Three Families of Onigafuchi Village... I have something to ask you."
Mion looked utterly lost at first, but... she reacted sharply to those final words—the next head of the Sonozaki family.
"...............Where did you hear that?"
"...That... doesn't matter."
The one thing I couldn't reveal was that Takano-san had told me.
...You told me not to breathe a word of it, Takano-san... but forgive me.@k...This is exactly why I sought you out in the first place.
"...I'm surprised you even know the name 'Onigafuchi Village,' Kei-chan...@k@vS03/03/140300179. ...but the stuff about me sounds like quite an earful."
"...Yeah.@k@vS03/01/140100406. ...A mix of truth and fiction.@k@vS03/01/140100407. ...I might just be making a huge misunderstanding based on some baseless rumor."
"I don't know what you're about to say, Kei-chan, but... let me be clear first.@k@vS03/03/140300181. ...Whatever it is, I have no authority. I can't grant anything you ask for."
"...So ideally, I'd rather not hear it at all.@k@vS03/03/140300183. Even if I do, there's nothing I can do about it."
"...............But... if talking about it will lighten your burden even a little, I'll listen.@k@vS03/03/140300185. ...I'll just listen, though. That's all I can do."
Mion... seemed to already know what kind of conversation this would be.
She warned me upfront that it would be futile.
...That much was obvious.
...Because if what I was about to say was true... the Sonozaki family would be... the masterminds behind the serial mysterious deaths that plagued Hinamizawa every year.
"...Oyashiro-sama's curse... it's real, isn't it?"
Mion gave a small nod.

"...............No one knows what it is or why it happens.@k@vS03/01/140100413. ...Even if each incident can be explained on its own, together they form a chain that can't be dismissed as coincidence."
"...But there's one thing they all have in common.@k@vS03/01/140100415. And that's... the target is always chosen from among the village's enemies."
Mion spoke as if it had nothing to do with her.
...That was fine.
She didn't need to admit anything.@k...For now, I just needed her to stay quiet and listen.
"...The day after tomorrow is Watanagashi, right?@k@vS03/01/140100417. ...This year's curse victim... has that already been decided?"
Mion didn't answer.@kHer expression didn't change, either.
...True to her word... she simply sat and listened in silence.
"Oh, sorry.@k@vS03/01/140100419. ...You promised to just listen... so it's strange for me to be asking questions, huh?"
Mion wore an expression I'd never seen from her before—something resigned, something pitying... something completely unfamiliar.
...She didn't nod... just listened with the faintest trace of a smile.
"If... this year's curse... isn't going to fall on Satoko's uncle... then please change it to him.@k@vS03/01/140100421. ...Change the target."
"...Ahahaha.@k@vS03/03/140300190. I see—that's one way to look at the order of things."

"I mean, after her aunt died from the curse last year, the guy abandoned Satoko and ran off to the city for a whole year!@k@vS03/01/140100424. ...He only came back recently—and for all the wrong reasons!"
"...You're right.@k@vS03/03/140300192. If he died from the curse this year, nobody would find it strange."
"I... I heard something.@k@vS03/01/140100427. ...That Oyashiro-sama's curse is actually... well..."
Beyond this point... no matter how I looked at it... should I really say it?
...No matter how I tried... the words... wouldn't come.

"The string of mysterious deaths in Hinamizawa,@k@vS03/03/140300194. ...commonly known as 'Oyashiro-sama's curse,' is perpetrated by the Three Families under the leadership of the Sonozaki house... isn't it?"

"I-I'm not...!!@k@vS03/01/140100429. I don't care whether you're involved in it or not!@k@vS03/01/140100430. ...If you are, that's fine with me!@k@vS03/01/140100431. I'll help you hide the evidence! I'll cover for you!"
"...I just want you to tell them... to choose Satoko's uncle as this year's target...@k@vS03/01/140100433. ...Please!!!"

"...............What you've been saying, Kei-chan, is truly awful.@k@vS03/03/140300196. If the serial deaths in Hinamizawa were indeed organized by the Three Families under the Sonozaki family's direction...@k@vS03/03/140300197. ...then what you're saying implicates me as well, you know?"

"It would mean I chose the curse's targets, gave the orders, killed who knows how many people, and made them disappear.@k@vS03/03/140300198. ...........Is that the kind of person you think I am, Kei-chan?"

"...That's not what I said!!@k@vS03/01/140100435. Weren't you listening?!@k@vS03/01/140100436. ...I don't care about that!@k@vS03/01/140100437. That doesn't matter at all!@k@vS03/01/140100438. Even if you killed a hundred people, you're still my friend!!"

"It doesn't matter one bit!@k@vS03/01/140100439. If the police come for you, I'll shield you!@k@vS03/01/140100440. If you need somewhere to hide, I'll hide you!@k@vS03/01/140100441. I'll lie through my teeth to protect you! I'll burn whatever evidence needs burning!@k@vS03/01/140100442. ...So please...!! Just this once... choose Satoko's uncle as the target...!!"
Mion... was speechless for a while.@k...She was simply stunned.
"...........................You're declaring yourself my accomplice, just like that?@k@vS03/03/140300200. Kei-chan, that's incredibly bold."
"Stop changing the subject!!!@k@vS03/01/140100444. ...Satoko's uncle—make him this year's—"
"Kei-chan, stop.@k@vS03/03/140300202. ...I need to start preparing dinner soon, so let's call it here for today."
Before I knew it... tears were streaming down my cheeks.@k...They ran down my chin... and fell in heavy drops onto the tatami.

"...As I said from the start.@k@vS03/03/140300204. ...You're operating under a huge misunderstanding, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300205. ......Oyashiro-sama's curse is Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS03/03/140300206. Even if there were people behind those incidents, it has nothing to do with the Sonozaki family, and even less to do with me."
"...Yeah... I know that, but..."
"...........Your desperate plea moved me, Kei-chan... but that's all it was.@k@vS03/03/140300208. ...No matter how deeply you feel, I have nothing... to give you in return."
"...But I understand your feelings now, so well it hurts.@k@vS03/03/140300211. ...If I really were... the person you seem to think I am..."
"...........I'd probably grant your wish."
"But reality is different. I'm just Mion Sonozaki."

"...True, the Sonozaki family has all sorts of bad rumors attached to it about controlling Hinamizawa from the shadows.@k@vS03/03/140300215. ...And we do hold a certain degree of influence—I won't deny that."
"...We may have gotten a bit extreme during the dam war, but that's the extent of it.@k@vS03/03/140300217. ...Murder is an entirely different matter.@k@vS03/03/140300218. ...That's the truth."
"...Don't say... such sad things...@k@vS03/01/140100450. ...Just for now, at least...@k@vS03/01/140100451. ...even if it's a lie... please just say you'll do it.@k@vS03/01/140100452. Please..."

"Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300219. You're free to dream.@k@vS03/03/140300220. If it would comfort you, I could lie and tell you I'll do it.@k@vS03/03/140300221. ...I could put on a front, like I'm some mighty figure."

"...But reality is merciless—Satoko's uncle will survive past the night of the festival.@k@vS03/03/140300222. ...He'll still be alive next week, and the week after that.@k@vS03/03/140300223. That's what's going to happen."
"...I don't want you coming to me the day after Watanagashi, demanding to know why I didn't kill him."
Mion rose slowly to her feet and slid the shoji shut to block the chill of the evening air outside...

"...Was it Miyo-san, by any chance?@k@vS03/03/140300226. Who put all these ideas in your head, Kei-chan?@k@vS03/03/140300227. ...The only person who could know that much and not be from around here... it'd have to be her."
"...I wish she wouldn't go influencing you like this, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300230. ...Good grief.@k@vS03/03/140300231. ...That woman doesn't know where to draw the line."
"Takano-san... has nothing to do with this."
"...All right, all right.@k@vS03/03/140300233. But don't worry."
"Even if I knew Miyo-san told you, nothing would happen.@k@vS03/03/140300235. ...She studies folklore. That's all.@k@vS03/03/140300236. ...She's not the type to meddle in real affairs."
"...Besides, we don't even know if the curse will happen this year.@k@vS03/03/140300237. ...Those incidents are just unfortunate coincidences."
"...........Thank you for listening.@k@vS03/01/140100456. ...If you really have nothing to do with the serial incidents... then I'm sorry for what I said."
"I see.@k@vS03/03/140300239. ...You'll apologize to her for me?"
"...Hahahahahahahaha."
...Mion said she didn't have much time, but she still walked me all the way to the gate.
She told me there was a planning meeting today for Sunday's Watanagashi festival, and that a crowd of neighborhood association officers and committee members would be arriving soon.
...Even on such a busy day, she had taken the time to listen to me.
"...If there's a God in heaven...@k@vS03/03/140300242. ...he surely understands how strongly you feel, Kei-chan."
"..................Yeah.@k@vS03/01/140100459. I'm really sorry for saying such bizarre things today.@k@vS03/01/140100460. ...I'm serious about the apology."
"...Okay.@k@vS03/03/140300244. I'll forget all about it."
"All right.@k@vS03/01/140100462. ...See you tomorrow."
"Bye-bye."
My time with Mion... had been truly brief.
...But talking to her had given me something to think about.
...One thing was certain: what Takano-san had told me was not a complete fabrication.
If Mion truly had no connection to the incidents whatsoever, she probably wouldn't have listened to me so solemnly.
...The fact that she heard me out to the end without dismissing me or making jokes... I wanted to believe that was proof enough.
...I was grasping at straws, and I knew it.
Thinking back on it... it was an appalling thing to have done.
I'd barged into my best friend's house, called her a serial murderer, and demanded she kill Satoko's uncle—@kall completely one-sided.
Mion had sat there calmly and listened to everything, like a priest hearing a confession.
...She could have lost her temper halfway through, told me to go wash my face in miso soup, and thrown me out.
...But she didn't.
Was that proof that Mion's family, the Sonozakis, were truly the masterminds?@k...Or had she simply taken pity on me, half out of my mind as I was, and listened quietly?
Regardless... it was one of the few efforts I could still make.
A last-ditch contingency plan.
Literally grasping at straws... a completely futile act of desperation.
Sensei had said that once she reported it to the child consultation center, they would respond that same day.
...Could they already be in the middle of it right now...?
If a healthy, smiling Satoko showed up at school tomorrow morning... then all of this would have been needless worry.
If only tomorrow would come sooner...
That night was unusually hot and humid... and I couldn't fall asleep.
...I'd said all that to Sensei.@k...Surely they wouldn't decide to 'wait and see' again this year.
Satoko... how was she spending tonight?
...Had she felt the nightmare ending, savoring a night of liberation?
I actually clasped my hands together... and prayed from beneath my futon.
"G'morning, Keiichi-kun!"

"Thank goodness.@k@vS03/02/140200116. I thought you'd overslept again today!@k@vS03/02/140200117. Yesterday, your mom said you'd already left, but then you never showed up at school.@k@vS03/02/140200118. I was worried you'd gotten heat stroke on the way!"
"Yeah, yeah, I'm terribly sorry for all the worry I caused yesterday."
"Heeey, good morniiing!"
"Mii-chan, good morning~!"
"...Are you okay? You look really, really sleepy."
"Come to think of it, didn't you have a late-night meeting with the town council people last night?"
"'Meeting' is generous—it was basically a party.@k@vS03/03/140300248. We've been doing the festival every year, after all."
"There's nothing complicated left to discuss at this point."
"Are you going to help with the festival setup this afternoon?"
"Yep.@k@vS03/03/140300251. I have to go around greeting people on Grandma's behalf and all that.@k@vS03/03/140300252. It's a hassle."
Our morning conversation was lighthearted, as always.
...That alarming talk I'd forced on Mion at her house yesterday... she seemed to have taken me at my word and forgotten it.
...The more I thought back on it, the more I realized what a terrible thing I'd said.@k...It wouldn't have been strange for her to scream at me, or worse.
There was a strong chance Takano-san had been exaggerating.
...The idea that Mion, our friend and comrade,
was the young leader of the Sonozakis—one of the Three Families that effectively ruled Hinamizawa—and the puppeteer behind the serial mysterious deaths... was honestly hard to believe.
...But... if there were even a one-in-a-million chance... that it was true...
...then what I'd said to Mion yesterday... might bear fruit.
Yes, it was truly one in a million.
...But even the smallest effort to raise the odds of saving Satoko, even by a fraction...
...At the very least, I had made that effort.
...As of yesterday, I'd done everything within my power.
...If there was anything else I could do... it would probably just be standing under a waterfall and praying for Satoko's uncle to die from the curse...
"...I hope Satoko comes today."
"...Me too.@k@vS03/02/140200123. ...Sensei arranged everything, didn't she?@k@vS03/02/140200124. Hmm. ...I wonder how it went."
"The welfare commissioner for the Hinamizawa district is actually my aunt.@k@vS03/03/140300254. ...I called her last night to ask about it."
"Huh, I didn't know your aunt was a welfare commissioner, Mii-chan."
"...So what happened?@k@vS03/02/140200128. What did she say about Satoko-chan?"

"Well.@k@vS03/03/MIO040401. After we left, Sensei called the Okinomiya child consultation center directly.@k@vS03/03/140300257. And the center contacted the local welfare commissioner to gather information.@k@vS03/03/140300258. ...That's where my aunt comes in.@k@vS03/03/140300259. It seems she helped coordinate things."
"...Skip the procedural stuff.@k@vS03/01/140100467. What happened to Satoko?"
"Hmm...@k@vS03/03/140300260. My aunt wasn't told the details beyond that.@k@vS03/03/140300261. ...I was surprised too—she didn't know much."
changeReason: New translation omits that the information was 'supposed to be secret' and that the aunt telling Mion was 'a big breach of ethics.'

TextENGNew: "...........................Damn it, now I'm even more anxious."
"...Let's just... go to school."
Right.
No amount of discussion would do anything but feed our anxiety.
...Going to school and seeing for ourselves whether Satoko was safe was far more efficient.
The three of us exchanged nods... and broke into a run.
...Was Satoko safe?
What had the child consultation center done?
I didn't care what it was—anything—as long as Satoko could live in peace from today on, as long as her normal days would return.
At the shoe racks by the entrance, Rena peered into Satoko's cubby.
"Oh... Satoko-chan's shoes are here! They're here!"
"Keiichi-kun, Satoko-chan came today.@k@vS03/02/140200132. Thank goodness...!"
"Her being here doesn't tell us anything.@k@vS03/01/140100471. We can't know anything until we see her face!"
We rushed to the classroom.
The classroom... was lively with its usual morning bustle.
"...Mii.@k@vS03/05/140500023. She's right here."
"My, what a racket so early in the morning.@k@vS03/04/140400094. Would you please quiet down a little?"
"Satoko-chan!@k@vS03/02/140200134. ...Are you... are you okay?"
Our whole group crowded around Satoko at once.
"So, Satoko.@k@vS03/03/MIO040404. At your house... well... what happened?"
"...Who called those people?@k@vS03/04/140400096. Honestly... it was quite the ordeal, you know."
"It was the teacher.@k@vS03/01/140100475. ...You were absent for so long—of course she was going to worry."
...Satoko muttered under her breath that she'd told them she was fine.
Just as I thought... she was still trying to endure all of this hardship entirely on her own.
"...It was a terrible nuisance for both my uncle and me.@k@vS03/04/140400098. Having all those people show up out of nowhere—honestly, what were they thinking?"
"Everyone was worried about you, Satoko-chan.@k@vS03/02/140200136. Please don't call it a nuisance..."
"Ugh, enough!!@k@vS03/01/140100477. Just tell me already!@k@vS03/01/140100478. What happened?!"
"Nothing happened.@k@vS03/04/140400100. Ohohoho, my cold got better, so I came to school. That's all there is to it.@k@vS03/04/140400101. My, my—what a fuss over nothing!"
...What... was Satoko saying...?
"What about, um... your uncle? What happened with him?"
"Oh, he acknowledged there had been some misunderstandings, and we both apologized properly."
Tug.
...Rika-chan grabbed the back of my collar and pulled me away.
"...W-What is it, Rika-chan?"
Without a word, Rika-chan kept pulling me until we were out in the hallway.
"...............Rika-chan... what... did that mean?!"
"...Satoko... said there was nothing wrong and sent them away."
No... somewhere in the back of my mind... I'd feared it might turn out like this.
Satoko refused to accept anyone's help... refused to hide behind anyone's back... and was determined to weather this ordeal alone.
...She wanted to prove to Satoshi that when he returned, she'd no longer need to cower behind his back.
...Chie-sensei arrived.@k...Her expression was anything but bright.
"...........Maebara-kun, if Satoko-chan herself says there's no problem, we have no choice but to take her at her word."
"Who decided that?!"
"...And... after thorough deliberation, that's the judgment the Okinomiya consultation center reached as well..."
"What the hell is that supposed to mean?! Sensei, didn't you promise yesterday that you'd absolutely do something about it?! What is this?!"
"...........Keiichi.@k@vS03/05/140500026. ...This is actually the third time.@k@vS03/05/140500027. The third time... that the consultation center was contacted about Satoko."
"Furude-san...!"
Sensei tried to stop her, but Rika-chan pressed on regardless.
"...Twice before?@k@vS03/01/140100489. ...I heard about the report the winter before last that ended with 'wait and see,' but... there was another time?"
"...The father who died—he was not Satoko's biological father."
"What...?@k@vS03/01/140100491. Rika-chan... what does that... what?"
I was blindsided by the sudden revelation.
...I had no idea what Rika-chan was trying to tell me... but it seemed crucial enough that I couldn't interrupt.
"...Satoko's mother remarried.@k@vS03/05/140500030. Satoko and Satoshi were her children from a previous marriage... so the father was their stepfather."
Satoshi had been a good child—obedient and easy to get along with.
...Their new father accepted and doted on him quickly.
But... Satoko was different.
She was defiant, dishonest, and constantly causing trouble.@k...Her stepfather couldn't stand her.
"...Even before she was placed with her uncle and aunt... Satoko's family life was far from healthy."
Of course, the stepfather hadn't been malicious at the start.
...At first, he must have tried to accept his new daughter.
But... young Satoko made no effort whatsoever on her end.
She kept her distance from her stepfather and never once opened her heart to him.
...She constantly tested him with transparent lies and pranks.

"...I wasn't in Hinamizawa back then, so I don't know the details firsthand...@k@vS03/22/141400075. ...But when I called the consultation center yesterday, they told me things about Satoko-chan's past."
"...What kind of things, Sensei?"

"...That Satoko-chan...@k@vS03/22/CHIE040404. had called the authorities on her parents—@k@vS03/22/CHIE040405. ...particularly her stepfather—@k@vS03/22/CHIE040406. and made false accusations of abuse."
"False accusations...?"
"...Satoko made a false report... to try to drive away the father she hated."
"...After the relevant agencies investigated...@k@vS03/22/141400081. ...they determined that the real issue wasn't the stepfather—it was Satoko-chan. She had... a problem."
"A problem with Satoko... What kind of problem?"
...Before remarrying, Satoko's mother had lived with several men in common-law relationships.
changeReason: Both are valid interpretations; old's 'didn't result in remarriage' more literally matches 再婚までに至らずとも, while new's 'before remarrying' implies eventual remarriage.

TextENGNew: ...From a very young age, Satoko had been forced to call at least two or three different men 'Father.'
At some point... Satoko's mischief began to escalate.
...She started deliberately spilling food, overturning plates... even throwing them.
She broke neighbors' windows. She shoplifted candy.
She scattered thumbtacks everywhere and set traps designed to cause serious injury.@k...Nothing like the harmless pranks she was known for now.
She told lies that were immediately seen through... and was scolded for it every single time.
...But she never once changed her behavior.
"...Please don't think of Satoko-chan as a problem child.@k@vS03/22/141400084. ...This was how a small, young Satoko-chan screamed for help, the only way she knew how."
Satoko had made false accusations of abuse against her father... and child psychology experts from public institutions had looked into it.
They found she had a form of emotional trauma... and declared that she required counseling.
"...Oh... so that's why... the winter before last... they decided to wait and see...?"
"...Yes.@k@vS03/05/140500035. The center still had all the old records on file...@k@vS03/05/140500036. ...so the same conclusion was repeated."

"...What the hell.@k@vS03/01/140100499. ......So what you're saying is...@k@vS03/01/140100500. ...Satoko... might be lying, so be careful.@k@vS03/01/140100501. ...So that's why... they're just... taking a wait-and-see approach...?!"
"...............It was the girl who cried wolf."
...The fable about telling one lie... and never being believed again.

"D-Don't give me that crap!!@k@vS03/01/140100503. The past has nothing to do with this!@k@vS03/01/140100504. ...That stuff with her dad was completely different! It's nothing like what's happening now!"
...Neither Sensei nor Rika-chan had a reply.
...According to Rika-chan, a representative from the Okinomiya consultation center had visited to carefully assess the situation.
And... Satoko, putting on the bravest smile she could muster, denied everything.
...Satoko was determined to endure without anyone's help...@k...Satoko wanted to wait for Satoshi at home...@k...So she flatly denied the truth and turned them all away.
...You fool, Satoko...
...........What... are you putting yourself through...?
Do you really think this charade of endurance... will actually bring Satoshi back...?
Stop it already.
...Satoshi... abandoned you.
...The proof is that he never came back to save you.
...So just stop.@k...Stop this pointless game of pretending to endure...
"...............Everything we just discussed—forget all of it.@k@vS03/22/141400090. Understood?@k@vS03/22/141400091. If any of this reaches Satoko-chan's ears, I will be very upset."
After delivering that warning, Sensei returned to the teachers' lounge...
"...Keiichi.@k@vS03/05/140500040. Please don't think of Satoko as some pitiful, helpless girl."
"...Satoko is living her life with all her strength.@k@vS03/05/140500042. ...Satoko is—"
"I know that!! She's our irreplaceable friend!"

"......Satoko is still doing her best.@k@vS03/05/140500044. ...Let's watch over her... just a little longer.@k@vS03/05/140500045. ...If something truly terrible happens... I will be the first to act."
"...........'After something terrible happens'—is that it?"
Anything more would have come out as a fight... so I swallowed my words and returned to the classroom.
Mion and Rena were chatting with Satoko as if nothing had changed.
...It was idle, trivial conversation.
Lighthearted, even fun—just like the old days.
...If... Satoko truly said she could still endure...@kthen maybe I should do as Rika-chan said and watch over her.
...Maybe anything I did would just be unwanted meddling.
